definition: Give reasons for, or account for, a fact, phenomenon, or outcome. Must include a cause-and-effect relationship.

expected_response.format: Cause → mechanism → effect chain. Can be brief (1-2 sentences) or extended depending on mark allocation.
do_not: Merely name or list (that is describing, not explaining)

mark_expectation: 1 mark per causal link correctly identified; full marks for complete chain
question_stem_examples: Explain why grapes grown in cool climates tend to have higher acidity.
Explain how malolactic fermentation affects the style of white wine.
mentor_hint: Use the structure: [Factor] → [mechanism] → [result in the wine]. For example: 'Cool temperatures slow ripening → less sugar accumulation, more malic acid retained → higher acidity in finished wine.'
